sanskrit: कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि । योगिनः कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वाऽऽत्मशुद्धये ॥
hindiTranslation: कर्मयोगी आसक्तिका त्याग करके केवल ममतारहित इन्द्रियाँशरीरमनबुद्धि के द्वारा केवल अन्तःकरणकी शुद्धिके लिये ही कर्म करते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Yogis perform action with body, mind, intellect, and the senses, giving up attachment, only for inner purification.
essence: Action becomes cleansing when attachment is removed.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A true practitioner acts with the body, mind, intellect, and senses, but without clinging to results. The purpose is inner cleansing, not possession.
simpleInsight: You can do the work without being owned by it.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield at Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen while Krishna explains how action can continue without bondage. The yogi uses body, mind, intellect, and senses, but drops attachment so the action itself becomes purification.
modernRelevance: You send the difficult message, close the laptop, and still feel the knot in your stomach. The verse says the act can be clean even when the outcome is uncertain.
translationLiteral: कायेन (by the body) / मनसा (by the mind) / बुद्ध्या (by the intellect) / केवलैः (by the merely) / इन्द्रियैः (by the senses) / अपि (also) / योगिनः (yogis) / कर्म (action) / कुर्वन्ति (perform) / सङ्गम् (attachment) / त्यक्त्वा (having given up) / आत्मशुद्धये (for inner purification)
insight: It shifts action from self-protection to purification, so effort becomes a practice instead of a grab for results.

quoteVariants.text: Act with body, mind, and senses. Leave attachment behind; let action purify you.
Your work can cleanse you, not just achieve things.
